2604.06812.title: AGSC: Adaptive Granularity and Semantic Clustering for Uncertainty Quantification in Long-text Generation
2604.06812.paper_summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) have demonstrated impressive capabilities in long-form generation, yet their application is hindered by the hallucination problem. While Uncertainty Quantification (UQ) is essential for assessing reliability, the complex structure makes reliable aggregation across heterogeneous themes difficult, in addition, existing methods often overlook the nuance of neutral information and suffer from the high computational cost of fine-grained decomposition. To address these challenges, we propose AGSC (Adaptive Granularity and GMM-based Semantic Clustering), a UQ framework tailored for long-form generation. AGSC first uses NLI neutral probabilities as triggers to distinguish irrelevance from uncertainty, reducing unnecessary computation. It then applies Gaussian Mixture Model (GMM) soft clustering to model latent semantic themes and assign topic-aware weights for downstream aggregation. Experiments on BIO and LongFact show that AGSC achieves state-of-the-art correlation with factuality while reducing inference time by about 60% compared to full atomic decomposition.
2604.06812.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）在長篇生成方面展現了令人印象深刻的能力，但它們的應用受到幻覺問題的阻礙。雖然不確定性量化（UQ）對於評估可靠性至關重要，但其複雜的結構使得在異質主題之間進行可靠聚合變得困難。此外，現有的方法往往忽略了中立信息的細微差別，並且在細粒度分解上面臨高計算成本的問題。為了解決這些挑戰，我們提出了AGSC（自適應粒度和基於GMM的語義聚類），這是一個針對長篇生成量身定制的UQ框架。AGSC首先使用NLI中立概率作為觸發器，以區分不相關性和不確定性，從而減少不必要的計算。然後，它應用高斯混合模型（GMM）軟聚類來建模潛在的語義主題，並為下游聚合分配主題感知的權重。在BIO和LongFact上的實驗表明，AGSC在事實性方面達到了最先進的相關性，同時將推理時間減少了約60%，相比於完整的原子分解。
2604.06812.author: Guanran Luo et.al.
2604.06812.authors: Guanran Luo, Wentao Qiu, Wanru Zhao, Wenhan Lv, Zhongquan Jian, Meihong Wang, Qingqiang Wu
